industryChallenges.summary: The airline industry faces unprecedented pressure on customer service operations due to high seasonal volatility, multi-language support requirements, and increasingly complex regulatory compliance. Post-pandemic, airlines are experiencing 40% higher support ticket volumes with reduced staff capacity.

topChallenges.impact: Summer peaks see 300% increase in support demand, leading to 4-6 hour wait times and 15-20% abandoned tickets
Serving 40+ European markets requires agent coverage across 15+ languages, creating staffing and quality challenges
Flight delays/cancellations generate instant ticket surges (500+ tickets in 30 mins), overwhelming support teams

industryChallenges.aiTrends: Leading airlines (Lufthansa, Air France) are deploying AI chatbots for routine queries (booking changes, baggage fees) achieving 60-70% automation rates. Budget carriers lag behind but face higher pressure due to thinner margins.

customerContext.internalNotes: Ryanair currently uses basic chatbot (appears to be custom-built, limited capabilities). Recent Zendesk expansion discussions noted interest in AI but concerns about implementation complexity.
customerContext.helpCenterInsights: Extensive FAQ coverage (200+ articles) but no AI integration. Help center is well-maintained but navigation could be improved.
customerContext.publicSentiment: TrustPilot: 2.1/5 (43,000 reviews). Common complaints: 'impossible to reach support', 'chatbot useless', 'wait times over 3 hours'. Reddit mentions frustration with refund process complexity.
painPoints: High ticket volume during disruptions (delays, cancellations)
Low customer satisfaction with current support channels
Complex refund and booking change processes

productRecommendations.overview: AI-powered virtual agents that handle routine customer queries automatically, with human handoff for complex issues. Built on Zendesk's platform, trained on your help center and historical tickets.
valueProposition: Automate 60-70% of routine queries (booking changes, baggage fees, check-in assistance) - handling 300K+ tickets annually
Instant response during disruption events (flight delays) - no queue times for basic updates
Native multi-language support across all 15 EU languages without additional agent hiring
Scale up/down instantly during seasonal peaks without staffing costs

potentialOutcomes.quickWin: 30-day pilot: Automate top 10 ticket types (booking confirmation, baggage allowance, check-in issues) - expect 40-50% deflection in first month
potentialOutcomes.mediumTerm: 6 months: Expand to complex workflows (booking changes, refunds with verification) - reach 65% automation rate, reduce average handle time by 35%
potentialOutcomes.longTerm: 12+ months: Full integration with disruption management - proactive notifications, automatic rebooking suggestions, 80% automation target

discoveryQuestions: What % of your tickets are repeat questions that could be automated? (booking status, baggage fees, etc.)
During your last major disruption event (delay/cancellation), how many tickets came in within the first hour?
What's your current cost per ticket? How would 60-70% automation impact your support budget?
Which ticket types cause the longest handle times for agents?
whiteboardingPrompts: Let's map your top 20 ticket types by volume - which could be fully automated vs. need human judgment?
Walk me through a typical disruption scenario - where would AI agents add the most value?
What does your seasonal staffing curve look like? Where would AI fill the gaps?

productRecommendations.overview: AI-powered quality assurance that automatically scores 100% of agent conversations against your quality standards, with custom prompts tailored to your business.
valueProposition: Identify inconsistent responses across 15 languages and 40 markets - ensure compliance with EU261 regulations
Detect churn risk signals in real-time (refund threats, competitor mentions) - escalate high-value customers
Automatic CSAT prediction before customer feedback - intervene on negative experiences early
Scale QA from 2% manual sampling to 100% AI coverage without adding QA managers

potentialOutcomes.quickWin: 30 days: Baseline current QA scores, identify top 3 agent coaching opportunities, deploy 5 custom prompts for EU261 compliance
potentialOutcomes.mediumTerm: 6 months: Improve AQS by 8-12 points, reduce churn-risk escalations by 25%, tie QA scores to agent performance reviews
potentialOutcomes.longTerm: 12+ months: Fully automated QA program with real-time coaching, predictive CSAT intervention, industry-leading quality scores

discoveryQuestions: What % of conversations do you currently QA? What's your target coverage?
What are your biggest quality gaps? (response time, empathy, compliance, resolution quality)
How do you identify churn-risk customers today? How quickly can you escalate?
What compliance requirements do you need to monitor? (EU261, data privacy, refund policies)
whiteboardingPrompts: Let's design 5 custom QA prompts specific to airline operations - what behaviors matter most?
What does a 'perfect' customer interaction look like for Ryanair? How would we grade it?
Show me a recent escalation that QA could have caught earlier - what signals should we watch for?

productRecommendations.overview: Real-time AI assistant for agents - suggests responses, surfaces knowledge articles, and provides coaching as agents type.
valueProposition: Reduce average handle time by 25-30% by surfacing instant answers as agents type
Improve consistency across 200+ help center articles - agents always cite correct policy
Onboard new seasonal agents 50% faster - AI coaching in real-time
Reduce escalations by 15-20% - agents get expert guidance without manager intervention

potentialOutcomes.quickWin: 30 days: Deploy to 50 agents, measure AHT reduction, identify top 10 knowledge gaps Copilot fills
potentialOutcomes.mediumTerm: 6 months: Roll out to all 1,000+ agents, integrate with disruption playbooks, reduce onboarding time by 40%
potentialOutcomes.longTerm: 12+ months: Full integration with QA scores, real-time agent coaching based on quality gaps, AHT reduction of 30%+

discoveryQuestions: What's your average handle time? What % could be saved with instant knowledge access?
How long does it take to onboard a new seasonal agent? What's the biggest training bottleneck?
What % of agent time is spent searching for answers vs. actually helping customers?
What are your most common escalation reasons? Could agents handle more with better tools?
whiteboardingPrompts: Let's walk through a complex ticket (refund with EU261 claim) - where would Copilot help the agent?
What does your seasonal hiring look like? How would AI-powered onboarding change your ramp-up time?
Show me your knowledge base - which articles do agents struggle to find?

competitiveIntel.competitorAIUsage: Lufthansa and Air France have deployed AI chatbots achieving 60-70% automation. EasyJet announced AI investment in 2024. Budget carriers (Wizz Air, Ryanair) lag behind despite higher cost pressure.

riskFactors: Budget constraints - Ryanair's cost-conscious culture may resist AI investment despite ROI
Implementation complexity during peak season - timing is critical
Change management - agents may resist AI tools if not positioned correctly
Current chatbot investment - may create sunk cost bias against switching to Zendesk AI
innovationDayTalkingPoints: Airlines with AI agents handle 300% seasonal spikes without hiring temporary staff
Every 1% improvement in automation saves Ryanair ~€2-3M annually in support costs
AI QA catches EU261 compliance issues before they become regulatory fines
Multi-language AI support costs 90% less than hiring agents across 15 languages
Disruption management: AI can respond to 500 customers in 5 minutes vs. 5 hours with agents
